events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide fail because double-tapped breakers non-listed violates NEC 110.3(B) use per listing/manufacturer instructions, also 408.36 maybe overcurrent protection. Risk high? Double-tapped breakers can cause loose connections, arcing, fire. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Work stage rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 80. Findings: breakers with multiple conductors not listed for multiple conductors; violates manufacturer instructions. Actions: remove double taps, install listed breaker rated for multiple conductors or add pigtail/approved connector, verify torque. Rationale. References NEC 110.3(B), 408.36, maybe 110.26 not directly unless clearance but scenario only double taps. Need include 200.4? 200.4 says terminal connections not used for more conductors than rated. Actually NEC 200.4 is about terminals for grounded conductors? Let's recall: 200.4 Terminal Connections. Alterations to grounded conductor terminals shall not permit more conductors than terminal is designed for. Hmm. But double-tapped breakers are line-side ungrounded. Still relevant to terminal ratings. Also 110.26 working clearance not applicable but reference material includes it. We can mention if clearance not evaluated. Need confidence high. Value 0. Sound source etc empty string/list? Schema says string for sound_source, event_time number, rate number, order list, workable list. We can set sound_source: "", event_time:0, rate:0, order:[], workable:[]. Percent complete estimate maybe 85. Let's craft JSON. Ensure no markdown.

events.text: {"decision":false,"risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":85,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Circuit breakers are double-tapped with conductors not listed for multiple terminations","Terminals are used beyond their listed/manufacturer rating, creating loose-connection and arcing/fire risk","Installation violates NEC requirements for listed equipment use and panelboard makeup"],"actions":["Remove all non-listed double-tapped conductors from breakers","Install breakers listed and rated for two conductors per terminal, or pigtail conductors to a single listed termination using approved wire connector","Verify proper conductor size, torque per manufacturer specifications, and labeling","Re-inspect before energizing"],"rationale":"Double-tapped breakers that are not listed for multiple conductors violate NEC 110.3(B) and terminal-use requirements. This creates a high risk of overheating, arcing, and fire because the termination may not properly secure both conductors. The panelboard makeup is therefore non-compliant and must be corrected before acceptance.","references":["NEC 110.3(B) - Use of Equipment","NEC 408.36 - Overcurrent Protection","NEC 200.4 - Terminal Connections"]}
